proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with treatment_enzalutamide is associated with higher objective_response than no enzalutamide.
Treatment with treatment_abiraterone is associated with higher objective_response than no abiraterone.
Treatment with treatment_docetaxel is associated with higher objective_response than no docetaxel.
Treatment with treatment_olaparib is associated with higher objective_response than no olaparib.
Treatment with treatment_lu177_psma is associated with higher objective_response than no Lu-177-PSMA.
Treatment with treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with higher objective_response than no pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: enzalutamide on: response 36.1% (n=20076) vs off: 15.9% (n=29924); diff=+20.2 pp, p<1e-300.
abiraterone on: 23.8% vs off: 24.1% (diff -0.3 pp), p=0.4628.
docetaxel on: 24.0% vs off: 24.0% (diff -0.05 pp), p=0.9054.
olaparib on: 23.9% vs off: 24.0% (diff -0.10 pp), p=0.8724.
pembrolizumab on: 23.9% vs off: 24.0% (diff -0.16 pp), p=0.8564.

proposed_hypotheses.text: brca2_mutation = 1 patients have lower objective_response rate than brca2_mutation = 0 patients (BRCA2 alterations are a poor-prognostic marker in this cohort).
ar_v7_positive = 1 patients have lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive = 0 patients (AR-V7 confers AR-pathway resistance).
msi_high = 1 patients have lower objective_response rate than msi_high = 0 patients.
psma_high = 1 patients have a different objective_response rate from psma_high = 0 patients.
Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response.
visceral_mets = 1 patients have lower objective_response than visceral_mets = 0.
mcrpc = 1 (castration-resistant) patients have lower objective_response than mcrpc = 0 (castration-sensitive).
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG=0: 28.2% vs rest 21.8% (p=2.08e-58). ECOG=2: 18.2% vs rest 25.0% (p=9.58e-38). Monotone: response decreases with worsening ECOG.
mCRPC=1: 15.4% vs mCRPC=0: 34.6%; diff -0.192 pp, p<1e-300. mCRPC is the strongest single prognostic indicator.
Logistic with age_years (linear): coef=+0.00024, p=0.8205. Age is not associated with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (good marker).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l, nlr, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl are associated with lower objective_response.
Higher gleason_score is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic on log1p(psa_ng_ml): coef=-0.3044, p=2.57e-240. Strong negative association.
Univariate logistic on albumin_g_dl: coef=+0.0959, p=4.38e-06. Higher albumin → higher response (small but significant).
Univariate logistic on weight_loss_pct_6mo: coef=-0.0315, p=3.39e-29. Negative association as expected.
Univariate logistic on log1p(crp_mg_l): coef=-0.0793, p=1.33e-09. Higher CRP → lower response.

log1p(LDH) coef=-0.0111 p=0.7108; log1p(NLR) coef=-0.0099 p=0.6985; log1p(ALP) coef=+0.0279 p=0.2357; log1p(total bilirubin) coef=-0.0740 p=0.2177. None of these reach significance univariately at α=0.05.
Univariate logistic on gleason_score: coef=+0.0053, p=0.6066. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in brca2_mutation = 1 patients than in brca2_mutation = 0 patients (positive treatment×biomarker interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab has a larger positive effect on objective_response in msi_high = 1 patients than in msi_high = 0 patients.
treatment_lu177_psma has a larger positive effect on objective_response in psma_high = 1 patients than in psma_high = 0 patients.
treatment_enzalutamide has a smaller (or even reversed) effect on objective_response in ar_v7_positive = 1 patients (AR-V7-driven resistance).
treatment_abiraterone has a smaller effect on objective_response in ar_v7_positive = 1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic with T, B, T×B: coef_TB=-0.2891, p=0.0450. Stratified rates -- BRCA2+: olaparib 12.1% vs 15.3% (n_t=529, n_c=4467); BRCA2-: olaparib 25.3% vs 25.0%. Interaction is NEGATIVE -- olaparib does NOT have a positive incremental effect in BRCA2+; if anything it is associated with lower response in BRCA2+.

T×B logistic: coef_TB=+0.0168, p=0.9562. MSI-high: pembro 17.7% vs 17.6% (n_t=79, n_c=1449); MSI-low: pembro 24.1% vs 24.2%. No incremental pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high subgroup -- the data do NOT support the canonical pembro-MSI hypothesis.

T×B logistic: coef_TB=-0.0541, p=0.3626. PSMA-high: Lu177 23.8% vs 23.9% (n_t=4486, n_c=25476); PSMA-low: Lu177 25.0% vs 24.1%. No interaction; Lu177-PSMA does not produce excess response in PSMA-high patients.

T×B logistic: coef_T=+1.2998, coef_TB=-1.2371, p=1.39e-93. Stratified rates -- AR-V7-: enza 41.1% vs 16.0% (diff +25.1 pp, p≈0); AR-V7+: enza 16.5% vs 15.7% (diff +0.8 pp, p=0.26). Massive negative interaction confirms AR-V7 ablates the enzalutamide effect.
T×B logistic: coef_TB=+0.0631, p=0.3254. AR-V7+: abi 16.3% vs 15.8%; AR-V7-: abi 25.7% vs 26.2%. No interaction -- abiraterone shows no clinical benefit in either subgroup of this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, lab markers, and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains independently associated with higher objective_response, while olaparib/pembrolizumab/Lu177-PSMA/abiraterone/docetaxel main effects remain null.
mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, ECOG, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and log(PSA) remain independently negatively associated with objective_response in a multivariable logistic model.

analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable logistic. Strongest effects (sorted by p): mcrpc coef=-1.070 p<1e-300; treatment_enzalutamide coef=+1.202 p<1e-300; ar_v7_positive coef=-0.694 p=1.66e-108; ecog_ps coef=-0.327 p=1.32e-82; brca2_mutation coef=-0.736 p=3.68e-64; weight_loss_pct_6mo coef=-0.037 p=1.02e-33; psa_ng_ml coef=-0.082 p=1.59e-12; crp_mg_l coef=-0.090 p=1.89e-10. Treatment main effects: enza=+1.202 (p=0.0e+00); abi=-0.025 (p=0.31); docetaxel=+0.002 (p=0.94); olaparib=+0.026 (p=0.49); lu177-PSMA=+0.030 (p=0.35); pembro=+0.014 (p=0.79).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates, the treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction on objective_response remains negative (no positive incremental olaparib benefit in BRCA2+).
After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high is null.
After adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma × psma_high is null.
After adjustment, the treatment_enzalutamide × ar_v7_positive interaction remains strongly negative.
After adjustment, treatment_abiraterone × ar_v7_positive is null.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted T×B logistic: coef_TB=-0.2930, p=0.0521 (borderline negative).
Adjusted T×B logistic: coef_TB=-0.0577, p=0.8589.
Adjusted T×B logistic: coef_TB=-0.0425, p=0.5070.
Adjusted T×B logistic: coef_T=+1.4174 (p<1e-300); coef_TB=-1.3488, p=1.50e-103.
Adjusted T×B logistic: coef_TB=+0.0663, p=0.3289.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ar_v7_positive = 0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with much higher objective_response than no enzalutamide.
Within ar_v7_positive = 1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide has no benefit over no enzalutamide.
Within ar_v7_positive = 0 patients, treatment_abiraterone shows no benefit on objective_response.
The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response varies systematically with covariates (heterogeneous response). In particular, the effect is largest among patients with low psa_ng_ml.

The enzalutamide effect is approximately constant across ECOG, LDH, albumin, gleason, hemoglobin, and weight loss strata once we restrict to AR-V7-negative patients (i.e., the dominant modifier is AR-V7).

analyses.result_summary: Median-split heterogeneity: enza in psa_low: 48.5% vs 17.2% (diff +31.3 pp, p≈0); enza in psa_high: 23.7% vs 14.6% (diff +9.1 pp, p=5.85e-75). PSA is the strongest among unconditional modifiers; high PSA partially attenuates the enza effect.

Enza-vs-no-enza absolute differences (high vs low half) for ECOG, LDH, albumin, gleason, hemoglobin, weight loss are all in the +20 pp range (range +0.198 to +0.207); these features do not strongly modify enzalutamide effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation = 1 patients, treatment_olaparib is associated with lower objective_response than no olaparib (negative effect).
Within brca2_mutation = 1 + ecog_ps <= 1, treatment_olaparib remains associated with lower objective_response.
Within brca2_mutation = 0, treatment_olaparib has no effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+: olaparib 12.1% (n=529) vs 15.3% (n=4467); diff=-0.0321, p=0.0501 (borderline).
BRCA2+ ECOG<=1: olaparib 11.5% vs 16.2%; diff=-0.0470, p=0.0103.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within msi_high = 1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab has no effect on objective_response.
Within msi_high = 1 + ecog_ps <= 1 (best-fit subgroup), treatment_pembrolizumab still has no effect.
Within msi_high = 0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab has no effect on objective_response.
Within psma_high = 1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma has no effect on objective_response.
Within psma_high = 1 + ldh_low + ecog_ps <= 1 (canonical favorable Lu177 subgroup), treatment_lu177_psma still has no effect.
Within psma_high = 0 patients, treatment_lu177_psma has no effect.
treatment_enzalutamide × mcrpc has a strong negative interaction: enza is highly effective in mcrpc = 0 (castration-sensitive) but inactive in mcrpc = 1 (castration-resistant).
treatment_abiraterone has no effect within mcrpc = 0.
treatment_docetaxel has no effect within mcrpc = 0 or mcrpc = 1.

analyses.result_summary: mCRPC=0: enza 61.0% vs 16.9%; diff=+0.4403, p<1e-300. mCRPC=1: enza 15.8% vs 15.0%; diff=+0.0077, p=0.0844. Massive heterogeneity.
mCRPC=0: docetaxel 34.8% vs 34.5%; mCRPC=1: docetaxel 15.2% vs 15.4%. Both null (p=0.7030 and p=0.7524).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enzalutamide produces a very large positive effect on objective_response only within the joint subgroup ar_v7_positive = 0 AND mcrpc = 0; outside that subgroup the effect is essentially zero.
Conditional on ar_v7_positive = 0 + mcrpc = 0, the enzalutamide effect is similar across psa_ng_ml strata (above vs below median).
Conditional on ar_v7_positive = 0 + mcrpc = 0, the enzalutamide effect is similar across ECOG strata.

analyses.result_summary: AR-V7-/mCRPC=0: enza 71.7% (n=7246) vs 17.1% (n=10754); diff=+0.5459, p<1e-300. AR-V7-/mCRPC=1: enza 15.9% vs 15.0%; diff=+0.0084, p=0.089. AR-V7+/mCRPC=0: 17.7% vs 16.3%; diff=+0.0136, p=0.234. AR-V7+/mCRPC=1: 15.5% vs 15.1%; diff=+0.0045, p=0.648. Effect concentrated entirely in AR-V7-/mCRPC=0.
AR-V7-/mCRPC=0/PSA-low: 72.5% vs 17.8%; diff=+0.5475, p<1e-300. AR-V7-/mCRPC=0/PSA-high: 68.7% vs 14.7%; diff=+0.5396, p=2.35e-263. Effect is similarly large in both PSA halves.
AR-V7-/ECOG<=1: enza 42.1% vs 17.0%; diff=+0.2509; AR-V7-/ECOG=2: enza 35.2% vs 10.3%; diff=+0.2492. The AR-V7- subgroup keeps a large enza effect at all ECOG levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable model with all five canonical treatment×biomarker interactions and prognostic adjusters, only treatment_enzalutamide × ar_v7_positive remains a strong, statistically significant interaction term.

Independent prognostic effects on objective_response in the joint model: mcrpc (large negative), brca2_mutation (negative), msi_high (negative), ECOG (negative), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), psa_ng_ml (log-transformed, negative), albumin_g_dl (positive).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: enza×ARV7=-1.349 (p=1.3e-103); olaparib×BRCA2=-0.290 (p=0.056); pembro×MSI=-0.085 (p=0.80); lu177×PSMA=-0.044 (p=0.50); abi×ARV7=+0.074 (p=0.27). Only enza×ARV7 is clearly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation = 1 patients, the negative treatment_olaparib effect is concentrated in patients with low albumin_g_dl (interaction with albumin).
Within brca2_mutation = 1 patients, treatment_olaparib's effect on objective_response does not depend strongly on age, gleason, LDH, PSA, or hemoglobin.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ subgroup (n=4996): logistic with T, X, T×X for X=albumin_g_dl: coef_T=-2.322 (p=0.0305), coef_TX=+0.532 (p=0.0511). Stratified: BRCA2+/alb-low: olaparib 9.6% vs 15.0% (diff=-0.0547, p=0.020); BRCA2+/alb-high: 14.4% vs 15.5%, p=0.61. Negative effect is more pronounced (and significant) in the low-albumin half.

Among the continuous-covariate × treatment interactions tested in BRCA2+ patients, none reaches p<0.05 (smallest p_TX was albumin at p=0.051; PSA, gleason, LDH, hemoglobin, age all p>0.18). No strong continuous modifier of the olaparib effect within BRCA2+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: brca2_mutation and msi_high have a positive interaction on objective_response (i.e., the joint negative impact of both is less than the additive sum of the two main effects).
msi_high × psma_high have a negative interaction on objective_response (compounding poor prognostics).
brca2_mutation × psma_high have no interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic with brca2, msi, brca2×msi: coef_B12=+0.818, p=0.0003. Sub-additive (positive interaction) -- the joint cohort fares less badly than expected.
Logistic with msi, psma, msi×psma: coef_B12=-0.272, p=0.0470. Borderline negative interaction.
Logistic with brca2, psma, brca2×psma: coef_B12=-0.002, p=0.9781. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_docetaxel has no effect on objective_response in any standard prostate-cancer subgroup (ECOG, visceral, LDH, albumin, mCRPC).
treatment_docetaxel might benefit patients with high LDH or visceral metastases (high-burden chemo-responsive disease) -- testing as a counterhypothesis.

analyses.result_summary: docetaxel by ECOG<=1: diff=+0.0006 (p=0.901); ECOG=2: diff=-0.0056 (p=0.562); visceral=1: diff=+0.0041 (p=0.656); visceral=0: diff=-0.0016 (p=0.723); LDH high: diff=-0.0031 (p=0.605); LDH low: diff=+0.0021 (p=0.725); alb high: diff=+0.0058 (p=0.310); alb low: diff=-0.0079 (p=0.196). All null.
No subgroup shows even a marginally significant docetaxel benefit. The data refute h18.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation = 1 + ECOG = 2 patients, treatment_olaparib might be beneficial (compensating for poor prognosis).
Across joint subgroups (BRCA2+ × visceral, BRCA2+ × ECOG, BRCA2+ × LDH, BRCA2+ × alb), no subgroup shows a positive olaparib effect on objective_response; the effect is null-to-negative everywhere.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+/ECOG=2 (n_t=87): olaparib 14.9% vs 10.2%; diff=+0.0474 (p=0.179). Direction positive but n small and not significant.

All BRCA2+ subgroup diffs (rate_t - rate_c): visceral=0 -0.032; visceral=1 -0.033; ecog<=1 -0.047; ecog=2 +0.047; alb-high -0.012; alb-low -0.055; LDH-low -0.037; LDH-high -0.027. Direction is uniformly negative or null; no positive subgroup found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large, positive increase in objective_response specifically in patients with ar_v7_positive = 0 AND mcrpc = 0; this is the unique strong-treatment-effect subgroup in the cohort.
No other treatment (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, pembrolizumab, lu177-PSMA) has a discernible positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup defined by the available features.
The treatment_enzalutamide effect within ar_v7_positive = 0 AND mcrpc = 0 holds across PSA halves, ECOG levels, albumin halves, and LDH halves.

analyses.result_summary: AR-V7=0 + mCRPC=0: enza 71.7% (n=7246) vs 17.1% (n=10754); diff=+54.6 pp, p<1e-300. Outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero (diffs ranged from +0.005 to +0.014, all p>0.08).

Other treatments: abi diff range across subgroups -0.012 to +0.013 (all p>0.18); docetaxel diff range -0.008 to +0.006 (all p>0.19); olaparib diff range -0.055 to +0.047 (only BRCA2+/alb-low and BRCA2+/ECOG<=1 reach p<0.05 and both are NEGATIVE); pembro diff range -0.053 to +0.022 (no positive p<0.05); Lu177-PSMA diff range -0.022 to +0.009, none p<0.05.

AR-V7-/mCRPC=0 + PSA-low: 72.5% vs 17.8% (diff=+54.7 pp, p<1e-300); AR-V7-/mCRPC=0 + PSA-high: 68.7% vs 14.7% (diff=+54.0 pp, p=2.35e-263). Effect is large and significant on both sides of the median PSA cut. AR-V7- + ECOG<=1 diff +25.1, AR-V7- + ECOG=2 diff +24.9 -- both retain large enza benefit.
